summary: Gruber highlights Apple's announcement of journaling support for HFS+, drawing attention to Apple's careful wording. He notes that Apple deliberately avoids calling it a 'journaling file system,' instead describing it as a journaling feature added to HFS+. He also points out that this capability is limited to Mac OS X Server, not the consumer version of the OS.
key_points: Apple added journaling capabilities to HFS+ but carefully avoided calling it a journaling file system
The distinction in terminology suggests Apple views journaling as a feature layered onto HFS+ rather than a fundamental redesign
The journaling feature was initially limited to Mac OS X Server only
money_quotes: Apple does not refer to it as a 'journaling file system', but rather as 'a robust new journaling feature for the Mac OS Extended (HFS+) file system'.
key_insight: Apple's precise language around HFS+ journaling reveals a deliberate strategy of incrementally improving an existing file system rather than replacing it.
